text: You are building a complex form in React with multiple interdependent fields. Which of the following approaches are suitable for managing the form's state efficiently?

options.A: Using multiple `useState` hooks for each individual input field.
options.B: Consolidating all form state into a single object managed by `useState`.
options.C: Implementing `useReducer` to manage complex state transitions and logic.
options.D: Storing form state directly in the DOM elements and accessing them via refs.

text: Consider the following Node.js code snippet. What will be the output order?

options.A: A, D, C, B
options.B: A, C, D, B
options.C: A, B, C, D
options.D: A, D, B, C

text: You are developing an Express.js application. Which of the following statements about middleware execution order are true?

options.A: Middleware functions are executed in the order they are defined with `app.use()`.
options.B: Route-specific middleware always executes before global middleware.
options.C: Error-handling middleware must have four arguments (`err, req, res, next`).
options.D: Middleware attached to a specific route path will only run for requests matching that path.

text: A MongoDB collection `users` has fields `email`, `isActive`, and `lastLogin`. You frequently query for active users who logged in recently, and also perform full-text searches on `email`. Which indexing strategies would be most beneficial?

options.A: Create a compound index on `{ isActive: 1, lastLogin: -1 }`.
options.B: Create a text index on `{ email: 'text' }`.
options.C: Create a single field index on `{ _id: 1 }`.
options.D: Create a partial index on `{ lastLogin: -1 }` where `isActive: true`.

text: You want to add a custom color `brand-purple` to your Tailwind CSS configuration. Where would you define this?

options.A: In `src/styles.css` using `@apply`.
options.B: In `tailwind.config.js` within the `theme.extend.colors` object.
options.C: Directly in your HTML as an inline style.
options.D: In a separate `custom-colors.js` file.

text: Consider a React component using `useEffect`. Which of the following statements about its dependency array are true?

options.A: An empty dependency array `[]` means the effect runs only once after the initial render.
options.B: Omitting the dependency array means the effect runs after every render.
options.C: Including a function from props in the dependency array can cause infinite re-renders if not memoized.
options.D: If a dependency array contains an object, changes to its properties are automatically detected.

text: In Node.js, what is the primary purpose of `async/await`?

options.A: To enable synchronous execution of asynchronous code.
options.B: To make asynchronous code look and behave more like synchronous code, improving readability.
options.C: To replace callback functions entirely for all asynchronous operations.
options.D: To provide a new way of defining event emitters.

text: You're building an Express.js API and want to implement robust error handling. Which practices are recommended?

options.A: Using a single global error-handling middleware at the end of your middleware stack.
options.B: Wrapping every route handler in a `try-catch` block.
options.C: Using an `async-handler` utility to catch errors from async route handlers and pass them to `next()`.
options.D: Sending a generic 'Something went wrong!' message for all errors to the client.

text: You have a MongoDB collection `orders` with documents like `{ _id: 1, customerId: 'A', total: 100, items: [{productId: 'X', qty: 1}] }`. You need to find the total revenue for each customer. Which aggregation pipeline stage would you use after `$match`?

options.A: `$lookup`
options.B: `$unwind`
options.C: `$group`
options.D: `$project`

text: When designing a responsive layout with Tailwind CSS, which of the following are valid approaches?

options.A: Using utility classes with prefixes like `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:` to apply styles at specific breakpoints.
options.B: Writing custom media queries directly in your CSS files.
options.C: Using the `container` class to set a fixed maximum width for content.
options.D: Defining all responsive styles using JavaScript based on `window.innerWidth`.

text: You need to manage application-wide state in a React application. When might you prefer using React's Context API over a state management library like Redux?

options.A: When the state changes frequently and requires complex asynchronous logic.
options.B: For simpler, less frequently updated state that needs to be accessible by many components.
options.C: When you need a predictable state container with time-travel debugging capabilities.
options.D: For passing down props to deeply nested components to avoid prop drilling.

text: Which of the following statements accurately describe Node.js Streams?

options.A: Streams are instances of `EventEmitter`.
options.B: Piping streams (`source.pipe(destination)`) automatically handles backpressure.
options.C: There are four fundamental stream types: Readable, Writable, Duplex, and Transform.
options.D: Streams are primarily used for processing small chunks of data in memory.

text: To enhance the security of an Express.js application by setting various HTTP headers, which popular middleware package would you typically use?

options.A: Passport.js
options.B: Helmet
options.C: CORS
options.D: Express-Validator

text: You are performing a multi-document operation in MongoDB that requires atomicity across several collections. Which conditions must be met to use transactions effectively?

options.A: The MongoDB deployment must be a replica set or a sharded cluster.
options.B: All operations within the transaction must be on the same database.
options.C: Transactions guarantee atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability (ACID).
options.D: Transactions are supported only on standalone MongoDB instances.

text: Which tool is primarily responsible for compiling your Tailwind CSS utility classes into actual CSS rules during the build process?

options.A: Webpack
options.B: Babel
options.C: PostCSS
options.D: ESLint

text: To optimize the performance of a React component, which of the following techniques can be employed?

options.A: Using `React.memo` for functional components to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
options.B: Wrapping event handlers passed as props with `useCallback`.
options.C: Moving all state management to a global context provider.
options.D: Memoizing expensive calculations within a component using `useMemo`.

text: What is the primary difference between `require()` and `import` (ES Modules) in Node.js?

options.A: `require()` is synchronous, while `import` is asynchronous.
options.B: `require()` is used for client-side JavaScript, `import` for server-side.
options.C: `require()` is for CommonJS modules, `import` is for ES Modules.
options.D: `require()` supports named exports, `import` only default exports.

text: You are organizing routes in a large Express.js application. Which of the following are good practices for modular routing?

options.A: Defining all routes in a single `app.js` file for simplicity.
options.B: Using `express.Router()` to create modular, mountable route handlers.
options.C: Separating routes into different files based on resource (e.g., `users.js`, `products.js`).
options.D: Passing the `app` object directly to route files for them to define routes.

text: You are designing a MongoDB schema for a `blog` with `posts` and `comments`. Each post can have many comments, and comments are rarely accessed without their parent post. Which schema design approach would be most appropriate?

options.A: Embedding comments directly within the `posts` document.
options.B: Storing comments in a separate `comments` collection and referencing the `postId`.
options.C: Using a two-way referencing approach where `posts` reference `commentIds` and `comments` reference `postId`.
options.D: Embedding comments if their number is relatively small and they don't grow indefinitely.

text: What is the primary benefit of Tailwind CSS's utility-first approach regarding CSS file size?

options.A: It generates a massive CSS file containing all possible utility combinations.
options.B: It allows for purging unused CSS, resulting in very small production CSS bundles.
options.C: It eliminates the need for any CSS files whatsoever.
options.D: It automatically compresses images referenced in CSS.

text: You are building a React application and find yourself repeating similar stateful logic across multiple components. Which of the following are valid solutions to abstract and reuse this logic?

options.A: Creating a higher-order component (HOC) to wrap components with shared logic.
options.B: Developing a custom Hook to encapsulate the stateful logic.
options.C: Using a render prop pattern to share logic via a function prop.
options.D: Copy-pasting the logic into each component that needs it.

text: Which of the following statements about Node.js `Buffer` objects are true?

options.A: Buffers are used to handle binary data directly.
options.B: Buffers are global objects and do not need to be imported.
options.C: Buffers are fixed-size allocations in memory.
options.D: Buffers are instances of JavaScript's `Array` class.

text: You are implementing user authentication in an Express.js application. Which of the following are common and secure practices?

options.A: Storing user passwords directly in the database as plain text.
options.B: Using `bcrypt` to hash passwords before storing them.
options.C: Implementing JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for stateless authentication in APIs.
options.D: Using server-side sessions to manage user login state.

text: What is the primary purpose of MongoDB Change Streams?

options.A: To perform real-time data analysis and aggregation.
options.B: To enable real-time applications to react to data changes in a collection or database.
options.C: To migrate data between different MongoDB instances.
options.D: To track schema changes in a collection.

text: In React, what is an Error Boundary and how does it function?

options.A: A component that catches JavaScript errors anywhere in its child component tree, logs those errors, and displays a fallback UI.
options.B: A special type of component that prevents errors from occurring in its children.
options.C: A utility function that helps debug errors in production.
options.D: A component that only catches errors thrown by asynchronous operations.
